payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claiming completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly describes claiming as the core strategic process performed by children on an ongoing basis.

payload.description: Defining task-based earning structure
payload.justification: SG916 articulates the parental process of defining tasks with associated monetary values.

payload.description: Distributing pocket money with accountability
payload.justification: SG919 describes distribution as a strategic process requiring reporting support for accountability.

payload.description: Task-based earning structure defining claimable work units with monetary values
payload.justification: SG915 describes tasks as strategic objects that children complete and claim, indicating a core entity in the enterprise model. | SG916 identifies the earning structure as a strategically important thing that parents define, representing a configuration entity.

payload.description: Weekly earnings as aggregated financial reward
payload.justification: SG918 specifies weekly earnings as a distinct strategic data object that children need visibility into.

payload.description: Historical record for longitudinal tracking
payload.justification: SG920 identifies historical records as strategically important retained information supporting tracking over time.

payload.description: Task completion tracked per child
payload.justification: SG917 describes the relationship between children and their personal task completion tracking.

payload.description: Weekly earnings accumulation cycle
payload.justification: SG918 specifies weekly as the strategic temporal cycle for earnings visibility and accumulation.

payload.description: Ongoing task claiming cycle
payload.justification: SG915 describes claiming as occurring on an ongoing basis, indicating a continuous strategic cycle.

payload.description: Child as task completer and claimer
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as strategic actors who complete tasks, track completion, and view earnings.

payload.description: Parent as task structure definer and distributor
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as strategic actors who define earning structures and distribute pocket money.

payload.description: Enable children to claim completed tasks and track task completion
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly expresses the core strategic purpose of enabling task claiming functionality. | SG917 articulates the strategic requirement for visibility supporting child awareness and tracking.

payload.description: Support accurate pocket money distribution accountability
payload.justification: SG919 articulates the strategic need for reporting to ensure distribution accountability.

payload.description: Enable longitudinal tracking through historical records
payload.justification: SG920 identifies the strategic purpose of historical retention supporting tracking over time.

payload.description: Provide earnings visibility for financial understanding
payload.justification: SG918 articulates the strategic need for children to understand their financial reward accumulation.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Compensated Contribution Framework
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining, valuing, and governing the structure through which contributions are recognized and compensated, establishing what work units are claimable and their monetary worth.

payload.name: Performance Recognition Entitlement
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to enabling participants to claim recognition for completed contributions and receive appropriate compensation, establishing the rights and mechanisms for asserting completed work.

payload.name: Financial Settlement Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to accurately aggregate, distribute, and account for financial rewards on a recurring basis, ensuring transparent and verifiable settlement of earned compensation.

payload.name: Performance Recordkeeping Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to maintain comprehensive historical records of contribution activity over time, preserving evidence of participant performance and enabling retrospective analysis.

payload.name: Financial Literacy Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to providing participants with clear visibility into earnings and compensation patterns to support understanding of financial outcomes and behaviors.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a task-based earning structure that defines claimable work units and their monetary values.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to providing the framework by which work units (tasks) are defined and valued; this is the core capability obligation derived from the What and How CCIs.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define and distribute tasks within the earning structure.
payload.rationale: The parent actor is accountable for creating and distributing the work units; this obligation surfaces from the Who-column CCI identifying the parent's role.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim completed tasks.
payload.rationale: The How/Process CCI describes claiming completed pocket money tasks; the Who/Actor CCI identifies the child as task completer and claimer; the Why/Goal CCI explicitly states enabling children to claim completed tasks. These converge on a single enterprise commitment to enable the claiming capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to track task completion.
payload.rationale: The Why/Goal CCI explicitly includes tracking task completion as a distinct capability alongside claiming. This is a separate obligation from the claiming function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain an ongoing task claiming cycle.
payload.rationale: The When/Cycle CCI establishes a temporal constraint that task claiming is an ongoing, continuous activity rather than a one-time or periodic event. This constrains the operational model at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall aggregate financial rewards on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to a recurring weekly accumulation cycle for earnings aggregation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for the distribution of pocket money.
payload.rationale: Enterprise-level governance obligation ensuring transparent and verifiable accountability for pocket money distribution.
payload.fit_criteria: Distribution records are maintained and auditable.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of task completions for each child to enable longitudinal tracking of participant performance.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to preserve comprehensive historical records combining What-entity (historical record), What-relationship (per-child tracking), and Why-goal (longitudinal tracking enablement)

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into earnings to enable financial understanding.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to making earnings information accessible to participants for the purpose of developing financial understanding.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks on an ongoing basis.
Articulates parental need to define task-based earning structure with monetary values.
Specifies child visibility requirement for task awareness and personal completion tracking.
Defines child need for weekly earnings visibility to understand financial reward accumulation.
Articulates parental reporting need to support accurate pocket money distribution accountability.
Identifies stakeholder need for historical record retention supporting longitudinal tracking purposes.
Identifies a section boundary introducing high-level conceptual description of the system.
Defines the core business concept of children claiming pocket money tasks upon completion throughout a weekly cycle.
Establishes the business entities of tasks with associated monetary values and parent authority to define them.
Specifies the conceptual relationship between completed tasks and the child entity who performed them.
Defines the information visibility concept for children to view both available and completed task entities.
Establishes the weekly reporting concept showing aggregated earnings as a derived business entity.
Defines parent visibility to child earnings and task completion reports to support the pocket money distribution business rule.
Specifies the weekly reset business rule where task availability state returns to unclaimed status.
Establishes the concept of temporal task data retention beyond the current operational week.
